meta.title: Link With Details
meta.description: A simple inner block with a headline, description, and tertiary button link. Designed to be used inside tab panels.

requiredPatterns.usage: Show optional description field when block is selected or has content
Sync list view name with headline content
Conditional classes for consecutive-block border styling

qualityChecklist: TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure
renameBlock() syncs headline to list view
Keyboard navigation works on the button link

attributes.name: anchor
attributes.description: HTML anchor for linking to this block

attributes.name: headline
attributes.description: Headline text displayed as an h3 heading at 24px (text-header-5)

attributes.name: buttons
attributes.description: Single tertiary button link. Always tertiary style. Only the button itself is clickable (not the entire block).
attributes.validation: Always exactly one button, always tertiary variation

wrapper.notes: gap-6 = 24px gap between headline, description, and button

wrapperConsecutive.element: div (conditional, applied when block follows another of the same type)
wrapperConsecutive.notes: border-top 1px charcoal, margin-top 32px (mt-8), padding-top 32px (pt-8). Use CSS adjacent sibling selector: .link-with-details + .link-with-details

headline.notes: 24px on mobile, scales to text-2xl (24px) on md+. Uses Anton heading font via text-header-5 utility. No italic (Anton rule).

description.notes: Regular paragraph text using body font (General Sans). Only rendered when non-empty.

buttonWrapper.notes: Wraps the ThemeButton. Only the button anchor is clickable, not the entire block.

button.notes: Always tertiary style. Includes arrow icon by default. Inherits hover/focus states from button.css.

mobile.behavior: Single column, full width. gap-6 (24px) between elements. Consecutive border/spacing still applies.

tablet.behavior: Same as mobile. No layout changes.

desktop.behavior: Same as tablet. Block is an inner block so its width is determined by the parent container (tab panel).

emptyState.headline: RichText placeholder shown when empty, block always renders wrapper
emptyState.description: Hidden when empty (shouldDisplay pattern in editor, conditional render in PHP)
emptyState.buttons: ThemeButton handles empty link state internally

consecutiveBlockStyling.notes: Applied via CSS rule in a custom stylesheet or via the block's own styles. The adjacent sibling selector ensures the first block in a group has no top border, and all subsequent blocks do. This works in both editor and frontend because WordPress generates .wp-block-takt-link-with-details as the wrapper class.

patterns.name: Link
keyboardInteractions: Enter: Executes the link and navigates to the target
ariaRequirements: Native <a> element with href provides the link role implicitly

button.ariaLabel: From button title text, optional aria-label override

decisions.decision: Use text-header-5 for headline (24px with Anton heading font)
decisions.evidence: Spec states 'heading size where the font is 24px'. text-header-5 = 1.5rem (24px) on mobile, text-2xl (24px) on md+. Anton font applied via the text-header-5 utility.
Spec states 'gap between elements of the block will be 24px'. gap-6 = 24px in Tailwind.

decisions.decision: Use mt-8 pt-8 (32px each) and border-t border-charcoal for consecutive blocks
decisions.evidence: Spec states 'border top of 1px, margin top and padding top of 32px' when same block type follows. mt-8 = 32px, pt-8 = 32px.

decisions.decision: Use CSS adjacent sibling selector for consecutive block styling
decisions.evidence: CSS adjacent sibling selector (.link-with-details + .link-with-details) cleanly handles the 'when same type follows' requirement without JavaScript or block context. Works identically in editor and frontend.

decisions.decision: Button always uses tertiary variation, no variation selector
decisions.evidence: Spec states 'always tertiary style'. No allowVariationChange in editor. Default array has one tertiary button.

decisions.decision: Block uses div wrapper (not section), no container class
decisions.evidence: Block is an inner block of TabPanel. It does not need section/container wrappers — those come from the parent Tabs block.
Block is an inner block used inside Tabs, which uses h2 for its ThemeHeading. The next level down is h3 for proper heading hierarchy.

decisions.decision: Only the button is clickable, not the entire block
decisions.evidence: Spec explicitly states 'only the button is clickable'.

decisions.decision: Use border-charcoal for the consecutive separator border color
decisions.evidence: border-charcoal is the standard border color throughout the theme (Accordion items, QuickLinks, Tabs, WideSocialMedia).
Spec says 'regular paragraph'. Body text in this theme uses font-sans (General Sans) at text-body size with font-medium weight per typography.css base styles.

variations.demoDescription: Only required fields populated. Tests empty state handling for optional fields.
All fields populated with realistic content.

attributes.headline: Undergraduate Programs
attributes.description: Explore our diverse range of undergraduate degree programs designed to prepare you for a successful career in your chosen field.

variations.demoDescription: Excessive text length to test overflow and wrapping behavior.

attributes.headline: This Is an Extremely Long Headline That Tests How the Block Handles Overflow When Content Exceeds Typical Lengths

buttons.title: Read the Full Details About This Program

variations.demoDescription: Multiple LinkWithDetails blocks stacked consecutively. Second and subsequent blocks should show border-top, margin-top, and padding-top.
Tests shouldDisplay() behavior when description is empty.
Simulates how the block looks when used as inner content of a tab panel, with multiple consecutive items.
